headline: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction That Expands Service Capabilities, Enhances Workday Solutions and Advances Industry Innovation
summary: CINCINNATI & WILMINGTON, Mass., March 11, 2026--Cintas Corporation (Nasdaq: CTAS) ("Cintas" or the "Company") and UniFirst Corporation (NYSE: UNF) ("UniFirst") today announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Cintas will acquire UniFirst for $310.00 per share in cash and stock, representing an enterprise value of approximately $5.5 billion.1

content.title: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction That Expands Service Capabilities, Enhances Workday Solutions and Advances Industry Innovation
content.summary: CINCINNATI & WILMINGTON, Mass., March 11, 2026--Cintas Corporation (Nasdaq: CTAS) ("Cintas" or the "Company") and UniFirst Corporation (NYSE: UNF) ("UniFirst") today announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Cintas will acquire UniFirst for $310.00 per share in cash and stock, representing an enterprise value of approximately $5.5 billion.1

headline: Cintas Agrees to Buy Uniform Maker UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Deal
summary: UniFirst shareholders will receive $155 in cash and 0.7720 shares of Cintas stock for each UniFirst share they own, according to a statement on Wednesday.  This represents a combined value of $310 per share based on Cintas’ closing price on Monday.

content.title: Cintas Agrees to Buy Uniform Maker UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Deal
content.summary: UniFirst shareholders will receive $155 in cash and 0.7720 shares of Cintas stock for each UniFirst share they own, according to a statement on Wednesday.  This represents a combined value of $310 per share based on Cintas’ closing price on Monday.

headline: Cintas to Buy Uniform Supplier UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Deal
summary: Cintas, a maker of workplace products, agreed to acquire UniFirst in a deal with an enterprise value of $5.5 billion, more than four years after it first bid for the smaller uniform supplier.

content.title: Cintas to Buy Uniform Supplier UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Deal
content.summary: Cintas, a maker of workplace products, agreed to acquire UniFirst in a deal with an enterprise value of $5.5 billion, more than four years after it first bid for the smaller uniform supplier.

headline: Cintas to Buy Uniform Maker UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Deal
summary: UniFirst shareholders will receive $155 in cash and 0.7720 shares of Cintas stock for each UniFirst share they own, according to a statement on Wednesday.  This represents a combined value of $310 per share based on Cintas’ closing price on Monday.  On Wednesday, UniFirst’s stock rose as much as 7.8% to $278.09, while Cintas fell 1.4%.

content.title: Cintas to Buy Uniform Maker UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Deal
content.summary: UniFirst shareholders will receive $155 in cash and 0.7720 shares of Cintas stock for each UniFirst share they own, according to a statement on Wednesday.  This represents a combined value of $310 per share based on Cintas’ closing price on Monday.  On Wednesday, UniFirst’s stock rose as much as 7.8% to $278.09, while Cintas fell 1.4%.

headline: Stocks to Watch Wednesday: Oracle, Rheinmetall, Campbell's
summary: ↗️ Oracle (ORCL): The cloud-computing company raised its sales outlook as demand for artificial intelligence continues to outpace supply. Shares rallied 10% premarket. ↘️ Rheinmetall (XE:RHM): The German arms maker's earnings fell short of expectations, sending shares down 5%.

headline: Why UniFirst (UNF) Stock Is Up Today
summary: Shares of workplace uniform provider UniFirst (NYSE:UNF) jumped 7.6% in the afternoon session after the company announced it had agreed to be acquired by its industry peer, Cintas, in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $5.5 billion.

content.title: Why UniFirst (UNF) Stock Is Up Today
content.summary: Shares of workplace uniform provider UniFirst (NYSE:UNF) jumped 7.6% in the afternoon session after the company announced it had agreed to be acquired by its industry peer, Cintas, in a cash-and-stock deal valued at approximately $5.5 billion.
